Home » State Senate District 29 — Republican Primary

State Senate District 29 — Republican Primary

This race is uncontested. Vincent Maietta is the only Republican on the primary ballot. He advances directly to November.

What a State Senator does: One of 35 senators in Augusta. Votes on state budget and all laws. Controls school funding, property tax relief, environmental regulations, healthcare policy, housing laws. District 29 covers Cape Elizabeth, South Portland, and part of Scarborough.

Vincent Maietta

Republican
South Portland · President, V&E Enterprises Inc. · Owner, Maietta Towing · Ballotpedia

Platform

No campaign website, social media, or public policy positions found as of publication date. Check VOTE411.org for League of Women Voters questionnaires.

Background

President of V&E Enterprises Inc. (South Portland). Owner/operator of Maietta Towing and Pleasant Hill Towing. Brother Robert runs Pleasant Hill Towing; both companies share equipment and participate in city tow rotation. LinkedIn shows involvement in affordable housing development (89 Elm Apartments, Portland, $87.8M project with Avesta Housing).

Perjury Case (2013)

June 7, 2013: Vincent and Robert Maietta each indicted on one Class C perjury count by a Cumberland County grand jury. A customer (Alex Anastasoff) was charged $1,425 for a tow that should have cost ~$190 under city rates. Investigation found Maietta Towing overcharged 4 additional customers ($1,060 total) in a 45-day period. City license suspended 5 months; fined $100. October 2013: DA dropped the perjury case — no conviction resulted. Press Herald, June 2013 · Press Herald, Oct 2013

Who You'd Face

Anne Carney (D) — Incumbent since 2020. Chairs Judiciary Committee. Sponsored foam container ban, pregnant worker protections, 2024 gun safety package. Strong environmental record. Full Democratic profile

Sources

Press Herald — Maietta perjury indictment, June 2013 · Press Herald — DA drops case, Oct 2013 · Ballotpedia — Vincent Maietta

Last updated: May 31, 2026.

South Portland Voter Guide — June 2026. Nonpartisan.

Sources: Maine Secretary of State, South Portland City Clerk.

This guide is an independent project. It does not endorse any candidate.
